commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: But they understood not this saying, and it was hid from them, that they perceived it not: and they feared to ask him about that saying. They understood not this word. They understood well enough what was meant by being delivered into the hands of his enemies, and being put to death; but they could not comprehend how Jesus Christ, whom they knew to be the Messias, and the Son of God, and whom they believed to be immortal, and eternal, could suffer death, or affronts and outrages from men. These ideas seemed incompatible; they perceived in them some mystery, which they could not penetrate.
